reasoning: With no search tools provided, I must rely entirely on base rates and my internal knowledge prior to my knowledge cutoff. The question requires 4 of the 7 Magnificent Seven stocks to experience a 50% decline from their all-time high between late 2023/early 2024 and Jan 1, 2026. Tesla is highly likely to fulfill this condition since it consistently traded below 50% of its late-2021 all-time high ($414) throughout early 2024. Nvidia is historically cyclical and could drop 50% from a new peak. However, securing two additional stocks from the relatively stable mega-caps (Microsoft, Apple, Alphabet, Amazon, Meta) falling 50% requires a massive macroeconomic shock or a 2000/2008/2022-style crash. Since major crashes with 50% drawdowns in defensive tech giants occur only about once every decade (and we just experienced one in 2022), a back-to-back 50% crash by 2025 is historically unlikely. Therefore, I estimate the probability of 4 of 7 crashing 50% to be very low, around 7%.
